goal: Lower default check phase1 threshold from 0.15 to 0.10 (current default misses true conflicts — int_60ba4f57 vs int_895e342e overlap 0.146, both touched engine/merge.go); add rapid PBT covering FingerprintOverlap symmetry/boundedness/monotonicity and CheckPrepare phase1 counting + threshold edge cases

summary.title: check phase1: lower default threshold 0.15→0.10 + add 8 PBT covering FingerprintOverlap and CheckPrepare
summary.what: Default phase1 threshold dropped to 0.10 in domain/config.go DefaultTeamConfig and this repo's .mainline/config.toml. New rapid PBT file internal/engine/check_property_test.go covering eight properties: FingerprintOverlap symmetry, boundedness in [0,1], self-dominance, monotonicity when adding a shared token to any dimension, empty-fingerprint-yields-zero; CheckPrepare count invariant (suspicious + below_threshold == eligible), threshold edge cases (0 → all suspicious, >1 → none), and task sort order (descending by overlap). Spec mainline-spec-v0.1-rc4-patch.md gained a §Patch 6 documenting the dogfood data, rationale, and the still-open follow-up of grid-searching the threshold against ≥50 real conflict cases.
summary.why: After PR #6 and #8 merged, ./mainline check --prepare --intent int_60ba4f57 produced 0 suspicious pairs because every cross-PR overlap fell below 0.15. Hand-computing the scores: int_60ba4f57 vs int_895e342e was 0.146 — 0.004 short of the gate, despite both PRs touching the same engine/merge.go file in the same engine subsystem with overlapping rc4/bugfix tags. That is the textbook conflict phase1 should catch. Two compounding root causes: (a) FingerprintOverlap had zero property tests, so weighting and jaccard regressions had nothing watching; (b) the spec's 0.30 default carried an explicit 'calibrate after 50+ real conflict cases via grid search' caveat that was never fulfilled, and the implementation's 0.15 was an unreviewed midpoint. False positives in phase1 are a few extra agent judgment tasks (cheap); false negatives are conflicts merged silently — the asymmetry argues for a more permissive default until calibration data exists.
summary.user_goal: Make `mainline check` actually fire on real cross-PR conflicts in this repo, and lock the algorithm down with PBT so future regressions are caught automatically.

decisions.rationale: On the 9-intent dogfood sample, 0.10 catches the target case (0.146) and only the next-closest one (0.112), keeping phase2 cost low while shrinking the false-negative rate that motivated the change.
rejected: Keep 0.15 — the value the bug was discovered with.
Drop to 0.05 — would surface 5+ false positives per check on this sample, defeating phase1's filtering role.
Tune the weighting algorithm itself — bigger refactor; the threshold is the cheap correct move first.

decisions.chose: All new properties in check_property_test.go with //go:build !quick
decisions.rationale: Keeps the existing TestJaccard / TestJaccardSymmetry / TestFingerprintOverlapEmptyVsFull intact (they exercise pure-function corner cases that don't need rapid) and concentrates rapid-driven properties in one file matching the project's existing convention.
Same release cycle, same subsystem; an entire new rc5 doc for one default value would over-weight the change. The §Patch 6 heading makes it obvious this section was added later.

risks: Threshold 0.10 is still a guess from a 9-sample dogfood, not a calibrated value. Phase1's job is to filter — if false-positive rate proves intolerable in practice teams can tune phase1_threshold up via team config, but the default biases toward catching conflicts.

PBT for FingerprintOverlap exercises set-based dimensions only; APIChange comparison (which uses signature strings) is exercised through the existing path but not specifically property-tested. Follow-up if APIChange comparison logic ever drifts.
followups: Collect 50+ real conflict cases over the next dogfood iterations and grid-search threshold against them.

Re-evaluate FingerprintOverlap's weighted-jaccard formula — single-dimension high overlap (e.g. same file_touched and same subsystem) gets diluted by other empty dimensions; consider max(weighted_avg, single_dim_above_strong_threshold).
PBT specifically for APIChange overlap comparison (signature string list).

architectural_claims: Phase1 default threshold 0.10 is the new floor; teams may tune up via [check] phase1_threshold once they have calibration data.
FingerprintOverlap and CheckPrepare semantics are pinned by 8 rapid PBTs covering the algebraic and structural invariants.
behavioral_changes: mainline check --prepare on this repo now flags int_60ba4f57 ↔ int_895e342e at score 0.146 plus int_60ba4f57 ↔ int_0e962db0 at 0.112; previously zero suspicious pairs.
migration_notes: Existing repos with phase1_threshold = 0.15 in their committed .mainline/config.toml are unaffected; only fresh inits pick up the new default. Teams who want the new floor in an existing repo should edit their config manually.
